proof:
obtain ⟨ k, hk ⟩ := h₁;
    -- We will prove that $k$ is a perfect square by induction on $a + b$.
    have h_ind : ∀ (a b k : ℕ), 0 < a → 0 < b → a^2 + b^2 = (a * b + 1) * k → ∃ t : ℕ, k = t^2 := by
      intro a b k ha hb hk
      induction' a using Nat.strong_induction_on with a ih generalizing b k
      induction' b using Nat.strong_induction_on with b ih' generalizing k
      by_cases h_case : a ≤ b
      -- Case 1: $a \leq b$
      by_cases h_case2 : b ≤ k * a
      -- Subcase 1a: $b \leq k * a$
      -- We can apply the same logic to $(b, k * a - b)$
      have h_ind_step : ∃ t : ℕ, k = t^2 := by
        by_cases h_eq : b = k * a;
        · exact ⟨ a, by subst h_eq; nlinarith ⟩;
        · contrapose! ih';
          use k * a - b, by
            rw [ tsub_lt_iff_left ] <;> try linarith [ Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne h_case2 h_eq ] ; ; nlinarith [ show k > 0 from Nat.pos_of_ne_zero fun h => ih' 0 <| by nlinarith ] ;, k, by
            exact Nat.sub_pos_of_lt ( lt_of_le_of_ne h_case2 h_eq ), by
            nlinarith only [ Nat.sub_add_cancel h_case2, hk ], by
            assumption
      exact h_ind_step
      -- Subcase 1b: $b > k * a$
      -- In this case, we have $k = a^2$.
      have h_k_a2 : k = a^2 := by
        nlinarith [ Nat.mul_le_mul_left a h_case ]
      exact ⟨a, by linarith⟩
      -- Case 2: $a > b$
      have h_symm : ∃ t : ℕ, k = t^2 := by
        exact ih b ( by linarith ) a k hb ha ( by linarith ) |> fun ⟨ t, ht ⟩ => ⟨ t, by linarith ⟩ ;
      exact h_symm
    obtain ⟨ t, rfl ⟩ := h_ind a b k h₀.1 h₀.2 hk; exact ⟨ t, by rw [ eq_div_iff ] <;> norm_cast ; nlinarith ⟩ ;
successful_attempts:
obtain ⟨ k, hk ⟩ := h₁;
    -- We will prove that $k$ is a perfect square by induction on $a + b$.
    have h_ind : ∀ (a b k : ℕ), 0 < a → 0 < b → a^2 + b^2 = (a * b + 1) * k → ∃ t : ℕ, k = t^2 := by
      intro a b k ha hb hk
      induction' a using Nat.strong_induction_on with a ih generalizing b k
      induction' b using Nat.strong_induction_on with b ih' generalizing k
      by_cases h_case : a ≤ b
      -- Case 1: $a \leq b$
      by_cases h_case2 : b ≤ k * a
      -- Subcase 1a: $b \leq k * a$
      -- We can apply the same logic to $(b, k * a - b)$
      have h_ind_step : ∃ t : ℕ, k = t^2 := by
        by_cases h_eq : b = k * a;
        · exact ⟨ a, by subst h_eq; nlinarith ⟩;
        · contrapose! ih';
          use k * a - b, by
            rw [ tsub_lt_iff_left ] <;> try linarith [ Nat.lt_of_le_of_ne h_case2 h_eq ] ; ; nlinarith [ show k > 0 from Nat.pos_of_ne_zero fun h => ih' 0 <| by nlinarith ] ;, k, by
            exact Nat.sub_pos_of_lt ( lt_of_le_of_ne h_case2 h_eq ), by
            nlinarith only [ Nat.sub_add_cancel h_case2, hk ], by
            assumption
      exact h_ind_step
      -- Subcase 1b: $b > k * a$
      -- In this case, we have $k = a^2$.
      have h_k_a2 : k = a^2 := by
        nlinarith [ Nat.mul_le_mul_left a h_case ]
      exact ⟨a, by linarith⟩
      -- Case 2: $a > b$
      have h_symm : ∃ t : ℕ, k = t^2 := by
        exact ih b ( by linarith ) a k hb ha ( by linarith ) |> fun ⟨ t, ht ⟩ => ⟨ t, by linarith ⟩ ;
      exact h_symm
    obtain ⟨ t, rfl ⟩ := h_ind a b k h₀.1 h₀.2 hk; exact ⟨ t, by rw [ eq_div_iff ] <;> norm_cast ; nlinarith ⟩ ;
